category_title: Disengaged students who exhibit off-task behaviors like iPad gaming and copying peers when frustrated
minimal_context: The student is taking an informal science assessment on their iPad in the classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open your iPads and start working on the informal science assessment.

STUDENT: Some of these questions were hard for me ’cuz I didn’t—like biodiversity, I didn’t know what it meant.

TUTOR: Just think about it and try your best to answer.

STUDENT: I try to remember. But if I can’t ‘Google’ the answer then I just don’t do it.

TUTOR: You need to finish the questions on your screen.

STUDENT: (Switches to playing a game on the iPad) I usually just wait for the answer. (Turns to peer) Hey, what did you put for this one?

options.A: Respond with enthusiastic praise for their progress on the assessment, saying, 'Another one already? No way! I’m so proud of you! Good job, keep going.'
options.B: Allow the student to withdraw from the classroom environment for a time-limited period of quiet time to prevent an emotional overload during the assessment.
options.C: Remind the student of the behavioral expectations, and when they continue to lose interest, dictate the answer to the assessment question for them.
options.D: Redirect the off-task student by engaging them in a specific content question, asking, 'Can you tell me something that is abiotic in this room?'
